etymology.description: semantic 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") + phonetic 殸(殸) (kēng, "stone chime") -> stone chime sound -> sound; voice
etymology.interpretation: The character 聲(shēng, "sound") combines the semantic element 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear"), suggesting the concept of hearing, with the phonetic element 殸(殸) (kēng, "stone chime"), which provides the sound of the word. The combination implies a sound that is heard, specifically the clear and resonant sound of a stone chime, leading to broader meanings of sound or voice.
etymology.simplification: 声(聲) maintains the same phonetic component 殸(聲), while the semantic component 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") is simplified, retaining its association with sound.

examples.chinese: 這件古老的樂器名叫聲石(這件古老的樂器名叫聲石)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn gǔlǎo de yuèqì míng jiào shēng shí.
examples.english: This ancient musical instrument is called the stone chime.

examples.chinese: 聲石在音樂中有特別的地位(聲石在音樂中有特別的地位)。
examples.pinyin: Shēng shí zài yīnyuè zhōng yǒu tèbié de dìwèi.
examples.english: The stone chime holds a special place in music.

examples.chinese: 每当演奏聲石时(每當演奏聲石時)，都有种神秘的气氛(都有種神秘的氣氛)。
examples.pinyin: Měidāng yǎnzòu shēng shí shí，dōu yǒu zhǒng shénmì de qìfēn.
examples.english: Whenever the stone chime is played, there is a mysterious atmosphere.
